breadcrumbs.id: threat hunting module/hunt me ii typo squatters/summary report
breadcrumbs.title: Summary Report

headings.text: 1- Perry’s haste causes him to install a malicious 7-Zip clone from a typosquatting site.
2- The user runs the fake 7-Zip installer.
3- The MSI stub immediately spawns PowerShell to fetch a hidden payload.
4- A legitimate 7-Zip installer is silently deployed to mask the attack.
5- A malicious back-door service is created.
6- The back-door loads a malicious DLL for code execution.
9- Mimikatz is staged and NT hashes stolen.
10- Hashes are used to impersonate high-privilege users.
14- The final destructive payload is deployed.

overview: 1- Perry’s haste causes him to install a malicious 7-Zip clone from a typosquatting site.

workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
